quick_skim.title: Modeling Adaptive Visual Search in Semantically Hierarchical Layouts
quick_skim.abstract: This paper introduces a computational cognitive model to investigate how information grouping impacts visual search, a key consideration in user interface design. The model uses computational rationality to view user behavior as an adaptation to cognitive and task constraints. Our work highlights that humans use hierarchical task representations, exploiting semantic and visual structures to improve search efficiency within the constraints of the visual system. We validate this model with data from two human studies focused on visual search and semantic categorization, demonstrating that semantic grouping improves search performance when it aligns with spatial grouping. Our model replicates task durations and eye movement patterns. By improving understanding of how hierarchical memory structures are utilized in human cognition, the model extends previous visual search models. We showcase our model in the rapid prototyping and evaluation of semantic visual groupings within user interface wireframes, suggesting a pathway toward applications in more complex, real-world interface design.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: computational model of visual search replicates eye movement patterns for understanding user goals
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 视觉搜索的计算模型复制眼动模式，用于理解用户目标
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 视觉搜索的计算模型复制眼动模式，用于理解用户目标
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 视觉搜索的计算模型复制眼动模式，用于理解用户目标
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper presents a computational cognitive model that replicates human eye movements during visual search in semantically hierarchical layouts, offering insights into how users exploit structure to achieve goals.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出一个计算认知模型，研究信息分组如何影响视觉搜索。模型采用计算理性框架，将用户行为视为对认知和任务约束的适应，成功复制了人类的搜索时长和眼动模式。实验表明语义分组在匹配空间分组时能提升搜索效率。该工作为理解用户通过眼动表达的目标提供了计算基础。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出一个计算认知模型，研究信息分组如何影响视觉搜索。模型采用计算理性框架，将用户行为视为对认知和任务约束的适应，成功复制了人类的搜索时长和眼动模式。实验表明语义分组在匹配空间分组时能提升搜索效率。该工作为理解用户通过眼动表达的目标提供了计算基础。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: computational models for understanding human goals through eye movements

quick_skim.title: Relational Gaze Transitions During Encoding Predict Episodic Recall of Naturalistic Scenes
quick_skim.abstract: Remembering a visual scene requires organizing distinct details into a cohesive event. This study investigates whether relation-guided gaze transitions provide a behavioural marker of this cognitive organization during episodic encoding and retrieval. By applying scene graph annotations to eye-tracking data, we measured whether gaze moved between objects that were meaningfully related within complex scenes. This approach allowed us to quantify relational scanning within naturalistic environments, moving beyond prior methods that relied on simplified displays or isolated relation types. Participants showed above-chance relational gaze during both initial viewing and blank-screen retrieval, indicating that gaze actively tracks scene structure during first viewing and at recall. Additionally, relational scanning at encoding predicted subsequent free recall of both object and relational details, even after accounting for salience, fixation frequency, meaning, and image-level differences. In contrast, relational scanning at retrieval did not predict recall success, suggesting that relational gaze is most functional to memory during its formation. Together, these findings show that relational gaze can be measured in complex scenes and may serve as a marker of episodic encoding during natural visual exploration.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Uses eye-tracking and scene graphs to model gaze transitions predicting recall, linking gaze to cognitive goals.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用眼动追踪和场景图建模眼动转换以预测记忆，将视线与认知目标关联。
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用眼动追踪和场景图建模眼动转换以预测记忆，将视线与认知目标关联。
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用眼动追踪和场景图建模眼动转换以预测记忆，将视线与认知目标关联。
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Shows that gaze transitions following object relations during encoding predict episodic recall; uses scene graph annotations on eye-tracking data to quantify relational scanning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文发现编码过程中的关系性眼动转换（即视线在场景中相关物体间的移动）能够预测后续的情景记忆表现。通过将场景图标注应用于眼动数据，量化了自然情境下的关系性扫描行为。结果表明眼动不仅是感知的产物，更反映了认知组织过程。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文发现编码过程中的关系性眼动转换（即视线在场景中相关物体间的移动）能够预测后续的情景记忆表现。通过将场景图标注应用于眼动数据，量化了自然情境下的关系性扫描行为。结果表明眼动不仅是感知的产物，更反映了认知组织过程。
